name: Pec Deck (Machine Fly)
resistance_profile: Machine adduction with a stable path; good stretch on the outer chest.
execution_steps: Set the seat height so the handles or pads sit at about chest height.
Sit back with your shoulder blades down and place your forearms on the pads (or grip the handles) with a slight, fixed elbow bend.
Let your arms open to a comfortable chest stretch under control.
Squeeze the pads together in front of your chest, then return slowly to the stretch.
good_when: You want a stable, easy-to-learn chest isolation with a loaded stretch.
You are adding fly volume without needing to stabilize free weights.
bad_when: You want to build pressing strength or overload heavily (press instead).
The machine does not fit your frame, forcing the shoulder into an awkward stretch.
